title: Migration, Release Gates, Rollback, Recovery, Operations, and Defense · Python Build Room
summary: A civic-publishing pair incrementally builds production publication, migration, and recovery dossier from original fixed content fixtures while retaining earlier Python, Git, HTML, CSS, URL, accessibility, security, determinism, testing, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of production publication, migration, and recovery dossier while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, c…
objectives: Compare old and new routes, redirects, canonicals, content identity, metadata, links, assets, accessibility, and reader tasks before cutover.
Gate one immutable artifact on content approval, correctness, accessibility, security, performance, reproducibility, portability, licenses, and operational readiness.
Retain prior artifacts and route manifests, rehearse host rollback, account for immutable asset and HTML cache behavior, and define abort thresholds.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Migration Diff
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop publishing team handles case 6085 during a project-subpath GitHub Pages deployment. Build deterministic Python evidence to defend migration, immutable release, cache-aware rollback, recovery ownership, and residual risk; record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, reader task, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner; then delete a source and change a shared layout, then compare incremental and clean artifact digests. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Migration Diff.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for compare old and new routes, redirects, canonicals, content identity, metadata, links, assets, accessibility, and reader tasks before cutover. For Static-site publishing and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Migration Diff, keep Migration Diff connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

options.text: State the Migration Diff preconditions, preserve earlier contracts, and name the trace or changed-input result that will decide success.
If every source file built, migration preserved the existing site. That claim fails the commit to the mission model evidence boundary for Migration Diff.

For commit to the mission model, render one trusted short page at the root, omit content authority, source spans, parser limits, route base, URL and asset reconciliation, contextual encoding, accessibility, clean-build comparison, deployment, rollback, recovery, and production transfer, then accept plausible HTML as publication proof. This does not satisfy Migration Diff for production publication, migration, and recovery dossier.

Browser code must not claim native filesystem, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validator, browser, GitHub, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN, cache, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, CommonMark 0.31.2 fixtures, markdown-it-py 4.2.0, Jinja 3.1.6 strict autoescape, pytest 9.1.1, standards validators, real browsers, clean package installs, reproducible artifacts, staged deployment, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-production-defense-1lhvxd
# Competency: ssg-production-defense.
# Case 5158: a multilingual public-service handbook migration.
# Operating constraint: test empty, malformed, deeply nested, Unicode, collision, unsafe URL, missing asset, stale cache, rejected, and recovery cases.
# Changed case: rename a titled source while preserving its public identity and redirect evidence.
def evidence_ssg_production_defense_1lhvxd(routes_before: int = 40, routes_after: int = 42, accounted_routes: int = 42, rollback_seconds: int = 90, recovery_owner: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1lhvxd = "1lhvxd-dxvhl1-5158"
    assert evidence_variant_1lhvxd
    evidence_axes_1lhvxd = (
        "reader1lhvxd",
        "source5158",
        "parsedxvhl1",
        "route1lhvxd5158",
        "artifact51581lhvxd",
        "recoverydxvhl15158",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1lhvxd)) == 6
    if routes_before < 1 or routes_after < 1:
        return False, routes_after, "migration-shape"
    if accounted_routes != routes_after:
        return False, accounted_routes, "route-diff-gap"
    if rollback_seconds < 0 or rollback_seconds > 300:
        return False, rollback_seconds, "rollback-budget"
    if not recovery_owner:
        return False, routes_after, "unowned-recovery"
    assert accounted_routes == routes_after
    return True, routes_after, "production-publication-defense"
